title: Fix passing arguments by reference (#3385 partial)
body:
It fixes passing function arguments by reference. Currently on master, they are handled as output arguments. This way, the argument passed by reference has always the default value at the beginning of the function.
In this PR, reference arguments are handled as `inout` arguments. It is correct in most of the cases. I think the only case in which these kinds of arguments shouldn't be handled in the same way is a case with forks.
Another difference is that `inout` arguments accept more types of expressions. Currently on master, only varrefs are supported in both `ref` and `inout` arguments. I added a support for all types that are allowed to be passed to `ref` argument, but only in non-inlined function. In a case with inlined functions var scopes are used, so probably a bigger change is required.
